english: — rtsi-tóg (rtsi tog) juicy fruit; rtsi-śiṅ (rtsi shing) 1. fruit-tree Pth. 2. tree, in gen. Glr. and elsewh., frq. — rtsi-gu (rtsi gu) fruit-kernel, the kernel in a fruit-stone (not the latter itself Sch.); W. for *tsí-gu* (*tsi gu*), q.v.; rtsi-gu-mar-nag (rtsi gu mar nag) oil extracted from the stones of apricots; rtsi-már (rtsi mar) Lt. id.

, pf. (b)rtsis ((b)rtsis), fut. brtsi (brtsi), imp. (b)rtsi(s) ((b)rtsi(s)) 1. to count, *sí-te bór-cè* (*si te bor ce*) W. to pay down, money; cf. also rtsis (rtsis). — 2. to count, reckon, calculate, mi ré-la p̣ul re-réi tạ́d-du (mi re la phul re rei tshad du) reckoning a handful to each Dzl.; źag súm-cu-la zlá-ba gcíg, zlá-ba bcú-gnyis-la lor rtsí-ba (zhag sum cu la zla ba gcig, zla ba bcu gnyis la lor rtsi ba) to reckon a month at 30 days, a year at 12 months Thgy.; mí-lo-ltar rtsí-ba (mi lo ltar rtsi ba) to count by the years of a man Thgy.; gaṅ bzaṅ rtsi-ba (gang bzang rtsi ba) to calculate which (day) be a propitious one Glr.; dus rtsi-ba (dus rtsi ba) to reckon up, to compute the time Mil.; *če-miṅ ḍál-la si-cè* (*che ming dral la si ce*) W. to reckon among the adults; yón-tan-la skyón-du rtsi-ba (yon tan la skyon du rtsi ba) to consider good qualities as faults, = ltá-ba (lta ba) I, 2; brduṅ rtsi (brdung rtsi) he may be reckoned to strike, i. e. he is very likely to strike, threatens to strike C.; brtsís zin (brtsis zin) 1. the account is closed, the bill is ready. 2. product, sum total.
n. of a plant, = pri-yáṅ-ku (pri yang ku) Wdn.

I. vb., pf. (b)rtsigs ((b)rtsigs), fut. brtsig (brtsig), imp. (b)rtsig(s) ((b)rtsig(s)), 1. to build, whether of stone or of wood, k´áṅ-pa (khang pa). — 2. to wall up, sgo (sgo) a door Glr. — II. sbst wall, masonry. Comp. rtsig-skyábs (rtsig skyabs) Stg. is said to be = rtsig-rmáṅ (rtsig rmang). — rtsig-ṅós (rtsig ngos) side of a wall. — rtsig-rdó (rtsig rdo) stone for building. — rtsig-dpón (rtsig dpon) master-mason, architect. — rtsig-p̣úr (rtsig phur) a peg in a wall, wall-hook, to hang up things. — rtsig-rmáṅ (rtsig rmang) fundament of a wall. — rtsig-zúr (rtsig zur) edge or ledge of a wall Thgy. — rtsig-bzó-pa (rtsig bzo pa) brick-layer, mason. — rtsig-yyóg (rtsig g.yog) journeyman mason.
Sch.: 'rtsigs-čě (rtsigs che) very gracious and well-affected' (?), prob. should be rtsis-čě (rtsis che) q. v. no. 3.
turbid matter, sediment, impurity, = tsigs-ma (tsigs ma) S.g.

adj. and sbst., coarse, clumsy, rough, rude; coarseness etc., B.; rtsiṅ-po (rtsing po) B. and C., rtsiṅ-ge (rtsing ge) C., W. id., but only adj; p̣ye (phye) coarse meal, grits (opp. to źib-po (zhib po), ⸰jám-po ('jam po)); spyód-pa rtsiṅ-ba (spyod pa rtsing ba) of rude manners Glr.
the long hair of the yak, rtsid-t´ágs (rtsid thags) = re-t´ágs (re thags) coarse cloth manufactured of it; rtsid-stán (rtsid stan) saddle-cloth Mil.; rtsid-gúr (rtsid gur) tent-covering made of it.

1. rib, rtsib-mai bár-nas (rtsib mai bar nas) from between the ribs Glr.; rtsib-lógs gyas gyon (rtsib logs g.yas g.yon) all the ribs of the right and left side Dzl; rtsib-logs ná-ba (rtsib logs na ba) pain about the ribs Do.; rtsib-riṅ (rtsib ring) the upper ribs (?) — 2. spoke of a wheel, frq.; rtsib-kyi mu-k´yúd (rtsib kyi mu khyud) fellies composing the rim of a wheel Cs.; in ornamental designs the rtsib-ma (rtsib ma) are often fanciful figures, supplying the radii of the circle; further: the sticks or ribs of a parasol, canopy etc. Glr.; the spars of a felt-tent, the ribs or futtocks of a boat Schr. — rtsib-ri (rtsib ri) n. of a mountain, = śri-ri (shri ri).

1. counting, numbering, numeration, rtsis-las ⸰das-pa (rtsis las 'das pa) innumerable Mil.; *bód si-la, món-si-la* (*bod si la, mon si la*) W. according to Tibetan, according to Indian counting or computation of time (is to-day the twentieth); *mí-si, ḍóṅ-si* (*mi si, grong si*) W. numbering of the people, of the domiciliated; *mág-si táṅ-cè* (*mag si tang ce*) W. to hold a numbering of military forces. — 2. account, rtsis byéd-pa (rtsis byed pa) Glr., ⸰débs-pa ('debs pa) Mil., gyáb-pa (rgyab pa) C., W. *kor-cè, (l)ta-cè* (*khor ce, (l)ta ce*) to calculate, to compute, rtsis-su sbyár-ḅa (rtsis su sbyar ba) to count together, to sum up Dzl.; calculation, computation (beforehand), scheme; *źag nyi-śu-la ⸰gro- (or ča-rtsis yod)* (*zhag nyi shu la 'gro- (or cha rtsis yod)*) W. in about 20 days we calculate, i.e. we intend, to go; *śiṅ-ta gyúg-si yód-pe dus-tsód-la* (*shing ta rgyug si yod pe dus tshod la*) Ld. at the hour, when according to their calculation the carriage was to start; rtsis-kyis (or rtsis byás-nas) ṅó-śes-pa (rtsis kyis (or rtsis byas nas) ngo shes pa) to find by computation Glr. — skár-rtsis (skar rtsis) astrology, astronomy; dkár-rtsis (dkar rtsis), nág-rtsis (nag rtsis), acc. to Cs.: Indian and Chinese astronomy and chronology. — 3. estimation, esteem, rtsis-po čén-po byéd-pa (rtsis po chen po byed pa) to value, to make much of, lús-kyi rtsis-po-čě (lus kyi rtsis po che) one that makes much of his own body, by indulging and adorning it Thgy.; rtsis-rtsis byéd-pa (rtsis rtsis byed pa) Sch. id.; dé-la bla- (de la bla-)
